title: Your Sleep Score Is Making You Worse at Sleeping
description: Consumer sleep trackers can't measure brain waves — the only reliable way to stage sleep — so they infer everything from movement and heart rate through proprietary algorithms nobody audits. A 2023 meta-analysis confirmed no wearable accurately classifies sleep stages against the clinical gold standard. But the deeper problem isn't the bad data. It's what people do with it: a growing clinical phenomenon called orthosomnia, where obsessive tracking of sleep scores actively degrades sleep quality. The device meant to optimize your rest may be the thing disrupting it.
